name: Paella
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the chicken into pieces and place in a pan to cook. Cover with water, add bay leaves, garlic chips, and parsley. Cook until done. Remove skin and bones, then cut into bite-sized pieces and return to the broth.
2. Place the shrimp in the pan and cover with water. Cook until done. Remove shrimp from water (retain water), peel, and devein. Strain shrimp water into a measuring cup for later use.
3. Use a Paella pan or electric skillet; heat oil. Add onion and saute until clear. Add rice and brown slightly. Add the remaining ingredients: chicken pieces, chicken broth, and shrimp. Bring to a boil and simmer until all water is absorbed.
When I describe this to friends, I say "it's rice and shrimp and chicken and lots of garlic" because I use lots of garlic in it. You can add as much chicken and shrimp as you want. I usually use only chicken breasts for my family. When Tita made it, she left the pieces whole and used more oil. She would throw weiners on top for the kids.
